content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886
July 17, 2000

Marilyn Mahaffy
4 Peninsula Avenue
Burlingame, CA 94010

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-00-125

Dear Ms. Mahaffy:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1 The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person’s authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(b)(1).) Because of these statutory and regulatory limitations, the Commission is not able to provide advice where the requestor is inquiring about another person’s duties under the Act and has not been authorized to do so by that person. (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B), copy enclosed.) Since your request relates to a Burlingame city councilmember and you are not the authorized representative for that individual, we are unable to provide you with advice. However, your concerns have been forwarded to the Commissioners.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel

By: Mark Krausse
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

LM:MK:klw

1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
